content.label: Identify sqrt(a^2 - x^2) -> x = a sin theta
content.applies_when: the integrand has the form sqrt(a^2 - x^2) with a = 2, which calls for the sine substitution x = a sin theta

content.meaning: let x = 2 sin theta so that dx = 2 cos theta dtheta, sqrt(4 - x^2) = 2 cos theta, and x^2 = 4 sin^2 theta

content.concept: sin theta = x/2, cos theta = sqrt(4 - x^2)/2
content.meaning: recover the triangle: sin theta = x/2, cos theta = sqrt(4 - x^2)/2, theta = arcsin(x/2)

content.label: Substitute and simplify
content.action: substitute x = 2 sin theta to get 4 integral sin^2 theta dtheta
content.purpose: clear the radical and reduce to a power of sine

content.label: Power-reduce and integrate
content.action: power-reduce: 4 integral (1 - cos 2theta)/2 dtheta = 2 theta - 2 sin theta cos theta
content.purpose: carry out the theta-integral using the double angle

content.label: Convert back from theta to x
content.action: use sin theta = x/2, cos theta = sqrt(4 - x^2)/2, theta = arcsin(x/2): 2 arcsin(x/2) - (x/2) sqrt(4 - x^2) + C
content.purpose: return the antiderivative to the original variable x
